title: SPY % Above 200-Day MA Breadth Gate — Market Participation Filter
summary: Compute the percentage of S&P 500 constituent stocks trading above their 200-day moving average using yfinance batch downloads on the S&P 500 index. When <40% of stocks are above their 200MA, suppress new long signals or reduce Kelly fraction; when >60%, allow full signal expression. This is one of the cheapest breadth-based regime indicators — it captures narrow leadership risk (e.g. the late-2024 Magnificent 7 concentration) and provides a mechanistic warning before index-level breakdowns. Recent Fidelity analysis (April 2025) confirmed that the AD line peaked in November 2024 while the index made new highs — exactly the divergence this filter would have caught.
expected_impact: Adding a 200-day MA filter to a trend-following strategy on SPY reduces max drawdown from 29% to 14% (halved) and improves win rate from 76% to 81% in a 1993–2026 backtest, at the cost of some CAGR (QuantifiedStrategies backtest evidence)
deferral_reason: Nice-to-have breadth gate; lower priority — deferred during paper_trading_single_account prioritisation
